title: 10 best herbal decoctions for oily hair
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for oily hair are a natural and effective way to manage excess oil production, reduce dandruff, and add shine to your locks. These liquid concoctions are made by steeping herbs like neem, tea tree, and chamomile in hot water, which helps to balance the scalp's pH level, reduce sebum secretion, and soothe irritations. For example, a decoction of neem leaves can help control oiliness while calming an itchy scalp, making it ideal for those with oily hair prone to dandruff. By using herbal decoctions, individuals can enjoy healthier-looking hair that's less greasy and more manageable, ultimately improving their overall confidence and self-esteem.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame decoctions helps with oily hair because it contains sesamin, a compound that reduces sebum production in the scalp. As a result, it helps to control oiliness and prevents clogged pores. The antioxidants present in sesame also help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to excessive oil secretion. Additionally, sesame decoctions have antifungal and antibacterial properties that prevent scalp infections, which can also contribute to oily hair.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sesame decoctions rich in antioxidants like sesamin and sesamolin help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oiliness, scaliness, and dandruff.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in sesame decoctions, such as sesamol, reduce inflammation on the scalp, which can help regulate sebum production and prevent oily hair.
Sebum-balancing properties: Sesame decoctions contain sesamin, which helps balance the production of sebum, regulating oil levels and preventing greasiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sesame roots are said to help balance excess oil production in the scalp, reducing dandruff and itchiness.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the sesame plant contain antioxidants that can help purify the scalp and reduce sebum buildup.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 20 grams of dried sesamum indicum leaves and flowers into a heat-resistant container.
Combine the measured sesamum indicum herbs with 200 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15 minutes, allowing the herbs to steep and release their active compounds.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before straining the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Transfer the final decoction into an amber glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 7 days, ready to use as a hair rinse treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe decoctions helps with oily hair because its natural compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, work to balance the scalp's oil production. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera soothe the scalp, reducing irritation that can cause excessive oil secretion. Additionally, aloe's humectant qualities help to absorb excess moisture, leaving hair feeling lighter and less greasy. Regular use of herbal aloe decoctions can help regulate sebum production, leading to a healthier, more manageable mane with reduced oiliness.
remedy_properties: Soothing and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Aloe's soothing properties can help calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily hair.
Antibacterial and Antifungal Properties: Aloe's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacterial and fungal overgrowth on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair and flaky skin.
Antioxidant Properties: Aloe's antioxidants can help neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and lead to oil production, helping to regulate sebum production and reduce oily hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the aloe plant are used to create medicated shampoos that help regulate sebum production, reducing oiliness and promoting healthy scalp balance.
Leaves: Aloe vera leaf extracts are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing itchy scalps and calming irritated skin prone to oil buildup.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from a healthy plant and cut them into small pieces.
Combine 100g of the aloe vera pieces with 500ml of distilled water in a large saucepan.
Boil the mixture over medium heat for 30 minutes to extract the herbal properties.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in an amber glass bottle at room temperature for up to 7 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage decoctions helps with oily hair because its antiseptic and astringent properties help to control excess oil production, reducing greasiness and impurities on the scalp. The herb's natural ability to balance pH levels also helps to normalize sebum production, leaving hair looking healthy and manageable. Additionally, sage's antibacterial properties combat dandruff and other scalp irritations, promoting a clean and refreshed scalp that absorbs less oil, resulting in healthier-looking locks.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage decoction helps combat bacteria and fungus on the scalp, reducing oil production and promoting a healthy balance of scalp flora.
Astringent properties: Sage's astringent compounds help to tighten the pores on the scalp, reducing excess sebum secretion and absorption, leading to less oily hair.

Antioxidant properties: Sage decoction's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and contribute to excessive oil production, promoting healthier and more balanced hair growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help to reduce oil production and soothe an itchy scalp.
Leaves: Sage leaves contain antioxidants and astringent compounds that help to balance the skin's natural pH, reducing excess oil and purifying the scalp.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one tablespoon of dried salvia officinalis herb and add it to a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Pour one cup of boiling water over the dried herb and let it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container to remove the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for at least 30 minutes.
Use the cooled decoction as a final rinse after shampooing and conditioning your hair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle decoctions helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction can help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing oil production and controlling sebum secretion. Additionally, the stinging nettle's ability to tighten pores prevents excess oil from entering the scalp, resulting in a reduction of greasiness. This natural treatment can also help to regulate the hair follicle's growth cycle, leading to healthier, less oily locks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as polysaccharides and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair.

Antioxidant properties: Nettle's rich antioxidant content, including vitamins C and E, helps protect the hair follicles and scalp from damage caused by free radicals, leading to a healthier and more balanced hair growth cycle.

Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in stinging nettle, such as quercetin and caffeic acid, help tighten and shrink the pores on the scalp, reducing oil production and preventing clogged pores.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in nutrients and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce dandruff and soothe scalp irritation caused by oily hair.
Leaves: Fresh or dried nettle leaves can be used to create a gentle, antifungal tea that helps to regulate sebum production and control oiliness.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried urtica dioica leaves and add them to a clean glass jar.
Boil 500 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan for five minutes.
Add the urtica dioica leaves to the boiling water and reduce heat to simmer.
Allow the mixture to steep for 20-30 minutes or until it has reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with oily hair because it stimulates blood flow to the scalp, reducing excess oil production. The antifungal and antibacterial properties of rosemary also help control yeast overgrowth on the scalp, which can contribute to oiliness. Additionally, rosemary's ability to balance the scalp's natural pH levels helps regulate sebum secretion, resulting in a reduction of oiliness and a healthier scalp overall. By using rosemary decoctions as a hair rinse, individuals with oily hair can achieve a cleaner, fresher, and more manageable mane.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair.

Stimulant and tonic properties: Rosemary's stimulant and tonic properties help to increase blood circulation to the scalp, which can stimulate hair growth and improve the overall health of the hair follicles, reducing oiliness.
Astringent properties: Rosemary's astringent properties help to reduce excess oil production on the scalp by constricting pores and regulating sebum secretion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rosemary roots help to reduce inflammation and soothe an itchy scalp associated with oily hair.
Leaves: The aromatic properties of rosemary leaves help to stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dandruff caused by excessive oil production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one tablespoon of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves or one teaspoon of dried leaves for this remedy.
Combine the harvested leaves with two cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Cool the decoction to room temperature and store it in an amber glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in the refrigerator for up to three days.
Shampoo your hair with the cooled decoction as you would a regular shampoo, focusing on oily areas and rinsing thoroughly with warm water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with oily hair because it has natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help to balance the scalp's natural oils. The decoction's gentle, soothing nature calms irritation and reduces oil production, while its antibacterial properties combat any underlying infections or fungi that may be contributing to excess oil. Additionally, lavender's antioxidant-rich compounds help to detoxify the scalp and promote healthy hair growth, leaving locks feeling clean, balanced, and manageable.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lavender's antimicrobial properties can help control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to oily hair and flaky scalps.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in lavender can help tighten and dry out the scalp, reducing oil production and excess moisture that can lead to greasy hair.
Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidant properties can help protect the scalp from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to inflammation and oily hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots contain bioactive compounds that help regulate sebum production, reducing oiliness and controlling dandruff.
Leaves: Lavender leaves have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe itchy scalps and reduce redness, making them effective in treating scalp irritations associated with oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning when essential oils are most concentrated.
Measure 20g of lavandula angustifolia flowers and add them to a medium-sized heat-resistant glass container.
Pour 500ml of distilled water over the herbs, ensuring that all flowers are submerged completely.
Bring the mixture to a simmer on low heat for 10-15 minutes, allowing the essential oils to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and storing in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with oily hair because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The astringent qualities of lemon balm help to balance the scalp's natural oil production, reducing excess sebum secretion and soothing irritated skin. This can lead to a decrease in oiliness and an improvement in overall hair texture. Additionally, lemon balm's ability to calm and purify the scalp can help to reduce dandruff and other scalp conditions that can contribute to oily hair. Regular use of a lemon balm decoction as a hair rinse can leave hair looking healthier and more manageable.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm's antimicrobial properties help to combat the overproduction of sebum on the scalp, reducing oiliness and promoting a healthy scalp environment.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in lemon balm help to protect the scalp from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oily hair.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and flakiness associated with oily hair, while also helping to regulate sebum production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have been traditionally used to reduce excess oil and purify the scalp, making them an effective ingredient in hair care recipes.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant are known for their astringent properties, which help to balance the skin's natural pH and reduce oil production.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one tablespoon of dried melissa officinalis herb and add it to a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Combine the herb with two cups of boiling water and let it steep for ten minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to five days.
Shake the mixture well and use one to two tablespoons as a final rinse after shampooing for best results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with oily hair because it has natural astringent properties that help to balance the scalp's oil production. The antioxidants present in dandelion also reduce inflammation and purify the blood, which in turn reduces sebum production and regulates hair's natural moisture levels. Additionally, dandelion's diuretic properties help to flush out toxins and excess water from the scalp, leaving hair looking healthy, shiny, and manageable with a reduced oiliness. This results in a healthier and more balanced scalp, reducing the need for frequent washing and styling products.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Dandelion helps to increase urine production, which can help to remove excess oil and impurities from the scalp, reducing greasiness and flakiness.
Antioxidant property: Dandelion's antioxidants can help to neutralize free radicals that can damage hair follicles and lead to oily buildup, promoting healthier hair growth and a balanced scalp.

Anti-inflammatory property: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to reduce redness and irritation caused by oiliness, soothing the scalp and preventing conditions like dandruff and psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their absorbent properties, dandelion roots help to balance oil production and purify the scalp.
Fruits: The astringent properties of dandelion fruits help to shrink pores and reduce sebum secretion, making them ideal for oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in an amount of one tablespoon per cup.
Boil filtered water at a rolling boil for five minutes then reduce heat to simmer.
Add the dried taraxacum officinale mixture to the hot water and let it steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids.
Let the cooled decoction sit in the refrigerator for at least two hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea decoctions helps with oily hair because they work to balance the scalp's natural oil production. Herbal teas like peppermint, chamomile, and green tea contain antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, which can contribute to oiliness. Additionally, the astringent properties in these teas help to tighten pores and reduce excess sebum production, leaving hair looking healthy, manageable, and less greasy. Regular use of herbal tea decoctions as a pre-shampoo treatment or as an after-shampoo rinse can help maintain a balanced scalp and hair.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Herbal teas like peppermint, chamomile, and witch hazel contain astringents that help to tighten the hair follicles, reducing oil production and absorb excess oil on the scalp, leading to a healthier and more manageable hair.

Antimicrobial properties: Teas containing herbs like tea tree, lavender, and rosemary have antimicrobial properties that help to combat scalp irritations, infections, and itchiness, which can contribute to oily hair and dandruff.

Soothing and anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal teas rich in flavonoids and polyphenols, such as green tea, hibiscus, and calendula, possess soothing and anti-inflammatory effects that calm irritated scalps, reduce inflammation, and promote a healthy balance of oil production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial properties, tea roots help to balance oil production and soothe an itchy scalp.
Barks: With its astringent properties, tea barks help to tighten pores and reduce excess sebum secretion, controlling oiliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried green tea leaves and grind them into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered green tea with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbal compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Use the cooled decoction as a final rinse after shampooing, massaging it onto oily hair for 1-2 minutes before rinsing thoroughly with warm water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with oily hair because of its natural astringent and antibacterial properties. The decoction helps to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing oil production and absorption. It also contains antioxidants that soothe and calm irritated scalps, which can contribute to excessive oiliness. Additionally, pot marigold has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and itchiness, promoting a healthy scalp environment that is less prone to oily hair. By incorporating this natural remedy into your hair care routine, you can say goodbye to greasy locks and hello to a balanced, manageable mane.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold contains compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, helping to reduce oiliness and prevent infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing redness and itching associated with oily hair, while also promoting healthy circulation and balancing sebum production.

Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold's antioxidant properties help protect the scalp and hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oiliness and dryness, as well as promote overall hair health and strength.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing dandruff and itchiness associated with oily hair.
Flowers: Packed with antibacterial properties, they help control oil production and soothe scalp irritations common in oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers or use dried flowers in amounts of one tablespoon per cup.
Combine the calendula flowers with two cups of boiling water in a saucepan and let it steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding the solids.
Add one teaspoon of apple cider vinegar to the liquid decoction and stir well for better absorption.
Allow the herbal calendula officinalis decoction to cool completely before storing it in the refrigerator for up to three days.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily hair with herbal decoctions is by creating a balanced concoction of herbs that target oiliness without stripping your scalp of its natural moisture. Try mixing equal parts of lavender, chamomile, and calendula flowers with boiling water, then let it steep for 10-15 minutes. Strain the mixture and use it as a final rinse after shampooing to help control sebum production and leave hair feeling refreshed and manageable.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily hair that are treated with herbal decoctions are acne, eczema, and hyperpigmentation. These conditions share similarities with oily hair in terms of excess sebum production and skin irritation. Herbal decoctions like neem, turmeric, and tea tree have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help to reduce inflammation, balance skin pH, and eliminate bacteria that contribute to these conditions.
